hypothesis: If CHROMA's encoder size and decoder depth are increased (e.g., from 1B/2 layers to 8B/8 layers), then the resulting designed multilayer stacks will achieve lower CIE76 ΔE color differences relative to target colors across training-paraphrase and validation prompts and across viewing angles (0°, 30°, 60°) because larger model capacity enables more accurate translation of natural-language prompts into material-thickness combinations.
